title: Local RAG Chat With Ollama And Nano
summary: Build a tiny Atlas-backed RAG chat flow using local nano embeddings and Ollama for generation.
prerequisites: Ollama is installed and running locally.
The `llama3.2:3b` model is already pulled in Ollama.
`vai nano setup` has already completed successfully.
MongoDB Atlas is configured through `MONGODB_URI` or `vai config set mongodb-uri ...`.
commands: Which models are used in this demo, and what benefits does the document mention?
What is the workflow from ingest to exit?

social.headline: A tiny Atlas-backed local RAG chat flow with Ollama and nano embeddings.
social.linkedinText: This VAI demo shows a strong end-to-end pattern for local RAG chat: ingest a small corpus, index it in MongoDB Atlas, embed locally with nano, and generate with Ollama. The gallery includes exact commands and the source tape so anyone can reproduce it.
social.xText: VAI demo: local RAG chat with Ollama + nano embeddings + MongoDB Atlas. End-to-end workflow, exact commands, and source tape included.
social.callToAction: Explore the workflow, then share the demo with your team.

voyageApi.node: // No remote Voyage AI API call.\n// This flow uses local embeddings plus Ollama for generation.
voyageApi.python: # No remote Voyage AI API call.\n# This flow uses local embeddings plus Ollama for generation.
voyageApi.curl: # No remote Voyage AI API call.\n# This flow uses local embeddings plus Ollama for generation.

explanations.vaiCommand: This is the high-level chat entrypoint from the GIF. VAI handles query embedding, Atlas retrieval, prompt assembly, and Ollama generation behind one command.
explanations.voyageApi: This demo is intentionally local on the model side: nano handles embeddings and Ollama handles generation. The point of transparency here is showing that no hosted Voyage AI API round-trip is happening.
explanations.mongoQuery: Atlas Vector Search is the retrieval layer for this chat flow. numCandidates is set higher than limit so the system can over-sample similar chunks before trimming the final context.
